report.executive_summary:
BUYER'S PLAIN-LANGUAGE OVERVIEW

Your purchase of 675 Bunker Hill Road, Columbia, MS cannot close right now due to a critical problem with the deed recording. Here's what you need to know:

The property deed that was recorded on January 13, 2026 names the wrong people as the new owners—it says Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson are receiving the property, when it should say YOU (Kyla B. Fortenberry) are receiving it. This is a serious mistake that must be fixed before you can get title insurance and close on your home.

Additionally, there are some questions about mineral rights on the property and some paperwork from the current owner (an LLC) that needs to be submitted. Once these issues are resolved, your purchase should be able to move forward.

Your readiness score is currently 67 out of 100. With the corrections outlined below, you should be ready to close.
report.property_info:
PROPERTY DETAILS

Address: 675 Bunker Hill Road, Columbia, MS 39429-7832
County: Marion County, Mississippi
Parcel Number: 074-04-000-04-005
Size: 88 acres
Assessed Value: $9,030 (land only)
Total Property Value: $60,190 (includes $34,010 in improvements)

The property consists of three separate parcels in Marion County that are being sold together as one transaction.
report.parties:
WHO'S INVOLVED IN THIS TRANSACTION

You (the Buyer): Kyla B. Fortenberry
Your Lender: Society Mortgage (they're lending you $80,808)
Current Owner: Garner Tree Farm, LLC (a Mississippi business)
Seller Listed on Documents: Terri G. Crawley (who is a member of the LLC)
Title Insurance Company: Old Republic National Title Insurance Company

Note: There's some confusion about whether the seller is the LLC or Terri G. Crawley individually. This needs to be clarified.
report.requirements:
WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN BEFORE CLOSING

1. ✗ CRITICAL - Fix the Deed: A new deed must be recorded that correctly names YOU as the buyer. The current deed (recorded January 13, 2026) names the wrong people and cannot be used.

2. ✓ Deed of Trust: Your lender's mortgage document needs to be recorded. This is standard and should happen at closing.

3. ✓ Bankruptcy Check: We've confirmed no bankruptcy filings exist for you, the seller, or the LLC.

4. ✓ Deed of Trust Verification: We've confirmed there are no other mortgages on the property that would interfere with your purchase.

5. ✗ LLC Paperwork: The current owner is an LLC, so we need to see:
   - A copy of the LLC's operating agreement
   - Proof that the LLC is in good standing with the state
   - A signed resolution from all LLC members approving the sale to you
   - Any other documents the title company requests after reviewing the LLC's records

6. ✗ Clarify Mineral Rights: The property has mineral rights that were separated from the land years ago (meaning someone else owns the oil, gas, and minerals underneath). We need to make sure the exceptions in the title commitment clearly explain what minerals are reserved and what you're actually getting.
report.exceptions:
WHAT'S NOT COVERED BY YOUR TITLE INSURANCE

Your title insurance will have standard exceptions for:

• Easements (utility rights): VYVX, Inc. has an easement on the property (recorded in 1997) and Bunker Hill Water Association has water line easements (recorded in 2009 and 2023). These allow them to access the property for utilities but don't affect your ownership.

• Mineral Rights: Someone else owns the oil, gas, and other minerals under the property. You're buying the land and surface rights only. This is common in rural Mississippi.

• Taxes: You'll be responsible for 2025 property taxes. The first installment ($1,132.52) has already been paid, and there are no delinquent taxes owed.

• Standard Exceptions: The policy will include typical exceptions for things like survey issues, liens that might appear later, and other standard title matters.

The main issue is that Exceptions 1 and 8 both mention mineral rights in slightly different ways, which creates some confusion. This needs to be cleaned up so it's clear what minerals are reserved.
report.risk_flags:
PROBLEMS THAT MUST BE FIXED

🚨 CRITICAL PROBLEM - Wrong Deed Recorded
The deed that was recorded on January 13, 2026 conveys the property to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson instead of to you. This is completely wrong and is the main reason your closing is blocked. A corrected deed must be recorded immediately with your name as the grantee (the person receiving the property).

⚠️ HIGH PRIORITY - Confusing Mineral Rights Language
The title commitment has two different exceptions (Exceptions 1 and 8) that both talk about mineral rights. They overlap and create confusion about exactly what minerals are reserved. The warranty deed does state that "all oil, gas, and other minerals" are excluded from what you're buying, but the exceptions need to be consolidated and clarified.

⚠️ MEDIUM PRIORITY - Seller Identity Confusion
The title commitment shows Garner Tree Farm, LLC as the owner, but Terri G. Crawley is listed as the seller. We need clarification on whether the LLC is selling the property or if Terri G. Crawley is selling it individually. The deed that was recorded shows the LLC as the grantor (seller), which is correct, but we need to understand why there's a discrepancy in the paperwork.
report.readiness:
CLOSING READINESS: 67/100

Your transaction is currently blocked and cannot close until the critical issues are resolved. Here's the breakdown:

What's Ready (✓):
- Your lender is approved and ready
- No bankruptcy issues exist
- No other mortgages are blocking the sale
- Property taxes are current
- Easements for utilities are standard and acceptable

What's NOT Ready (✗):
- The deed must be corrected (CRITICAL)
- LLC paperwork must be submitted
- Mineral rights exceptions must be clarified

Once these three items are completed, your readiness score should jump to 95+ and you'll be ready to close within days.
report.recommendations:
WHAT YOU NEED TO DO NOW

1. IMMEDIATE ACTION - Contact Your Real Estate Attorney or Closing Agent
Tell them the deed was recorded with the wrong grantee names. A corrected deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC to you (Kyla B. Fortenberry) must be prepared and recorded immediately. This is the biggest obstacle to closing.

2. Get the LLC Paperwork
Ask the seller or their attorney to provide:
   - The LLC's operating agreement
   - A certificate of good standing from Mississippi
   - A resolution signed by all four LLC members (Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, and Karen Garner Delancey) authorizing the sale

3. Clarify the Mineral Rights
Work with your title company to consolidate the two mineral rights exceptions into one clear statement so you understand exactly what minerals are reserved and what you're getting.

4. Confirm the Seller
Get written clarification about whether Garner Tree Farm, LLC or Terri G. Crawley is the actual seller. Based on the deed, it appears to be the LLC.

5. Timeline
Once the corrected deed is recorded and the LLC paperwork is submitted, closing should be able to happen within 5-7 business days.

BOTTOM LINE: This transaction can close, but the deed error must be fixed first. This is fixable, and once it's corrected, you should be able to move forward smoothly.
